rulings.Case Title: VATCHE CABAYAN M.D. VS. STATE COMPENSATION INSURANCE FUND et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiffs Vatche Cabayan M.D. And Vatche Cabayan Medical Corporation'S Motion For Terminating Sanctions And Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant For Continuous Abuses Of The Discovery Process And Ongoing Violations Of Court Orders And/Or For Order Compelling Further Responses To Requests For Production
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, October 26, 2012, Line 1, Plaintiffs Vatche Cabayan M.D. And Vatche Cabayan Medical Corporation'S Motion For Terminating Sanctions And Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant For Continuous Abuses Of The Discovery Process And Ongoing Violations Of Court Orders And/Or For Order Compelling Further Responses To Requests For Production. The court adopts the recommendation of Pro Tem Judge Halperin as follows: Granted. Within 15 days of service of the entry of order State Compensation Insurance Fund shall: (1) serve verified supplemental responses to requests for production 8, 10, 14, 15, 16 and 17, without objections based upon the investigation privilege, and explicitly stating that all nonprivileged documents responsive to those requests have been produced and that no documents have been withheld based upon a claim of the investigation privilege; (2) produce any non-privileged documents in the "A" and "D" range that have not been previously produced; (3) review all of the Fund's documents previously produced for emails that are missing attachments, and to produce any such emails again with their attachments; (4) re-examine every document listed in the Fund's privilege to ensure that it has not been withheld on a claim of investigation privilege, and produce unredacted copies of any that had either been redacted or withheld on the basis of a claim of investigation privilege; and (5) Serve a new privilege log on Plaintiffs' counsel that identifies authors, senders, or recipients who are attorneys as such, explicitly states the privilege being claimed for each document, and provides adequate description of the documents to fulfill the Fund's initial burden in claiming attorney-client or attorney work product privilege. Monetary sanctions of $ 5,000 are awarded to Dr. Cabayan and against State Compensation Insurance Fund. The court adds a payment due date of November 26, 2012. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: VATCHE CABAYAN M.D. VS. STATE COMPENSATION INSURANCE FUND et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Vatche Cabayan Medical Corporation'S Motion To Compel State Compensation Insurance Fund'S Further Responses To Form Interrogatories, Set 1 And Request For Admissions, Set 1; Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, October 26, 2012, Line 1, Vatche Cabayan Medical Corporation'S Motion To Compel State Compensation Insurance Fund'S Further Responses To Form Interrogatories, Set 1 And Request For Admissions, Set 1; Request For Sanctions. The court adopts the recommendation of Pro Tem Judge Halperin as follows: Denied. Plaintiffs did not obtain a written stipulation to extend their time to move to compel, the Court is without power to grant the motions. The Civil Discovery Act of 1986's time limits on motions to compel are "quasi jurisdictional" in the sense that they render the court without authority to rule on motions to compel other than to deny them. Sexton v. Superior Court (1997) 58 Cal.App.4th 1403, 1410. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: VATCHE CABAYAN M.D. VS. STATE COMPENSATION INSURANCE FUND et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiffs Vatche Cabayan M.D. And Vatche Cabayan Medical Corporation'S Motion By Plaintiff For Terminating Sanctions And Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant State Compensation Insurance Fund For Continuous Abuses Of The Discovery Process And Ongoing Violations Of Court Orders And/Or For Order Compelling Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set 1
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, October 26, 2012, Line 1, Plaintiffs Vatche Cabayan M.D. And Vatche Cabayan Medical Corporation'S Motion By Plaintiff For Terminating Sanctions And Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant State Compensation Insurance Fund For Continuous Abuses Of The Discovery Process And Ongoing Violations Of Court Orders And/Or For Order Compelling Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set 1. The court adopts the recommendation of Pro Tem Judge Halperin as follows: Granted. Within 15 days of service of the entry of order, State Compensation Insurance Fund shall serve verified code compliant supplemental responses to Plaintiff's Special Interrogatories Nos. 13, 17 through 21, and 40 through 48 without objections based upon the investigation privilege of Insurance Code Section 1877.4, and explicitly stating in each interrogatory response that no information has been withheld based upon a claim of the investigation privilege. Monetary sanctions are denied. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: CATHAY BANK, A CALIFORNIA BANKING CORPORATION VS. RAYMOND XIANG ZHANG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents Set One (Ccp 2031.310) And For Monetary Sanctions Against Pltf (Ccp 2023.010-2023.030, 2031.310(H))
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, October 26, 2012, at 9:00 am, line 2, DEFENDANT RAYMOND ZHANG'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents Set One (CCP 2031.310) And For Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff (Ccp 2023.010-2023.030, 2031.310(H)) Pro Tem Judge Katherine Huibonhoa, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =302/JPT

rulings.Case Title: NRT REO EXPERTS LLC VS. LAW OFFICES OF JOHN E BOUZANE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Request To Produce Documents; Memo Of P&A
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, October 26, 2012, at 9:00 am, line 3, DEFENDANT JOHN MARK EDWARD BOUZANE, INDIVIDUALLY AND DOING, BOUZANE, JOHN MARK EDWARD (SUED AS THE LAW OFFICES) Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Request To Produce Documents Pro Tem Judge Katherine Huibonhoa, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Denied. Moving party failed to provide courtesy copies of the moving papers in compliance with San Francisco Local Rule 2.6B. The Court continued this matter from October 16, 2012 with a request that moving party provide courtesy copies, but the Court still has not received any courtesy copies. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to katherinehuibonhoa@paulhastings.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/JPT

rulings.Case Title: RIVER PROPERTY TRUST VS. DAWN WIGGINS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Amended Cross Complaint Nunc Pro Tunc
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, October 26, 2012, Line 5, CROSS COMPLAINANT DAWN WIGGINS' Motion For Leave To File Amended Cross Complaint Nunc Pro Tunc. The Court denies this motion. The Court cannot approve of the filing of the so-called amended cross-complaint because that pleading has never been presented to the Court. Defendant may file a motion for leave to file the amended cross-complaint. On October 10, the Court gave plaintiff until November 15 to respond to the "cross-complaint." The Court extends that deadline until December 10, 2012. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, October 26, 2012, Line 8, DEFENDANTs LABORATORY CORPORATION OF AMERICA, LABORATORY CORPORATION OF AMERICA HOLDINGS' DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. The demurrer is sustained for causes of action 1, 2, 3, 6, 7, and 8 with 20 days leave to amend. The demurrer is overruled for causes of action 4 and 5.
